other_observers: First sighting was by Cathy Sheeter on May 1st, numerous unknown observers present on May 2nd
description: Small warbler shaped bird with completely black head, small thin bill, with thin incomplete white eye-ring.  Black coloring extended to back, wings, and central tail feathers.  Thick white wingbar present.  Throat was black.  Breast and belly were crimson red.  Flanks and undertail coverts were black.  Tail was long compared to where its primaries ended.  When the bird flew its outer tail feathers showed flashes of white.  Behavior was very flittery and bouncy.  It rarely stayed in the same spot.
